stories.headline: Germany halves GDP forecast from 1% to 0.5% due to Iran war fallout
stories.summary: The German government has officially halved its economic growth forecast for 2026, citing the severe impact of energy shocks stemming from the Iran war. This revision reflects a significant cooling for Europe's largest economy.

stories.headline: S&P Global Vice Chairman: Hormuz Crisis Is Driving Global Change
stories.summary:
Consumers are already feeling the effects of the closure of the Strait of Hormuz with energy prices surging around the world. But what if the crisis is signaling a longer-term global power shift?

On this episode of the Odd Lots podcast, S&P Global Vice Chairman Daniel Yergin tells Joe Weisenthal and Tracy Alloway that a "different world" will emerge, regardless of the war's ultimate outcome. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Oil Traders Say Billion-Barrel Hole Will Linger Long After War
stories.summary: The impact of the Iran war will continue for months even after any deal to restore shipping through the Strait of Hormuz, the world’s largest oil traders have warned. Some said flows through the waterway may never return to normal. Speaking at the FT Commodities Global Summit in Lausanne, executives at some of the world’s largest oil traders warned that the rewiring of the oil market would take months even if a peace deal is agreed soon.
